title: HTTP Services, Clients, Boundaries, Security, and Graceful Shutdown · Language Lens: Http Handler Routing
summary: A small service accepts hostile input, calls a slow dependency, serves concurrent requests, and must shut down without losing work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go http handler routing increment in a resilient secure HTTP service design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design Handler and ServeMux boundaries, method and path semantics, request limits, response status, headers, and streaming order.
Design command flags, stdin/stdout/stderr, exit status, signal cancellation, help, and machine-readable output as an interface contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Http Handler Routing · Theory Http Handler Routing
steps.instruction:
theory case — Service request 4 has a hostile label, oversized body, slow dependency, duplicate header, and client cancellation during shutdown. Bound resources, validate and encode by sink, preserve stable responses, and drain owned work without leaking internals.
